body: It looks like session_store and update_role draw separate connections from the pool, so the rotate and the role UPDATE aren't actually in the same transaction. A failure between the two would leave the user with a new role but stale tokens, or vice versa. Could rotate() take an optional connection argument so the caller can pass its own?
There's no test asserting that update_role() actually calls session_store.rotate(). Removing the rotate line from roles.py wouldn't break any test. Worth adding a test in test_roles.py?
